Hi Matthew,

Great to meet you virtually, and thank you for your patience while I was out of the office.

Could you please share the current status or confirmation from AWS regarding your DSA transfer request? The DSA transfer process can take up to three weeks, so we are probably looking at an April start date for partnership and transferring accounts, as we must have your DSA in place before completing the CTA account(s) transfer.

Additionally, we will need to get Aptum set up as a US TD SYNNEX partner with an account number and Net 30 credit terms. Please complete our TD SYNNEX Credit Application here: Become a Customer - TD SYNNEX USA and let me know if you have any questions.

I’m available for a call today if you’d like to walk through the process together.

Thank you,

 

Ciara Perkins
Multi-Cloud Business Development Representative III
(480) 936-5447
 
Reserve Time on My Calendar

 

From: Thomson, Christine <Christine.Thomson@tdsynnex.com>

To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Sankar, Rupesh <Rupesh.Sankar@tdsynnex.com>; Roodi, Maz <maz.roodi@tdsynnex.com>; Tammo, Gina <Gina.Tammo@tdsynnex.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Perkins, Ciara <ciara.perkins@tdsynnex.com>; Neumeier, Debra <debra.neumeier@tdsynnex.com>
Subject: APTUM/AWS CTA transfer 

 

+@Perkins, Ciara @Neumeier, Debra
 
Hi Matthew,
 
This is fantastic news~ Thank you for choosing to move forward with TD SYNNEX and for giving us the opportunity to earn your business. We are genuinely excited to support you & your team and ensure a smooth transition.
 
Rupesh, Maz, and I will remain fully engaged and dedicated to supporting all your needs for the Canadian region. In the US, Ciara Perkins (Business Development Rep, AWS)  and Deb Neumeier (Sr. Sales Manager) will be your go-to contacts, and they're ready to provide the white glove service Aptum needs to be fully onboarded. Ciara and Deb, I'm looping you in here so you can assist Matthew and the team in getting set up and operational in the US (CTA is requested for March 22). Your support will be invaluable in ensuring a seamless experience for everyone involved.  Rupesh will send a separate email with the background information to get you both fully up to speed on this opportunity.
 
In the meantime, I suggest we push ahead in Canada in getting the Aptum account activated and ready for future Canadian business. This way, you'll have everything in place and can hit the ground running as you expand.
 
Thanks again, Matthew—we truly appreciate the partnership and look forward to continuing a successful journey together.
 
 

Christine Thomson
Senior Director, Hyperscalers & ISVs
 

TD SYNNEX

From: Sankar, Rupesh <Rupesh.Sankar@tdsynnex.com> 
Sent: March 12, 2026 2:14 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Mohammadi, Maz <Mazyar.Mohammadi@tdsynnex.com>; Tammo, Gina <Gina.Tammo@tdsynnex.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Thomson, Christine <Christine.Thomson@tdsynnex.com>
Subject: RE: Placeholder Event: Preview of the new program through TD SYNNEX along with all the new incentives
 
Hi Matthew,
 
Please find the minutes from today’s call below.
 
***These are AI-generated minutes based on the discussion. Please review for accuracy.
 
Meeting Summary
 
AWS Partner Program Changes (Effective April 1st)
The meeting focused on major AWS partner program changes being rolled out, with Gina (TD SYNNEX subject matter expert) presenting a comprehensive overview. Key changes include a new base discount structure, deprecation of several legacy incentives, and new operational requirements. The changes represent a significant overhaul of how AWS incentivizes channel partners, with many updates piling up simultaneously affecting TD SYNNEX's ~4,900 global partners.
Timeline of Changes:
- January 1st: PGI started; PGD and other incentives deprecated
- February 1st: All RPPA changes went into effect
- April 1st: New base benefit, deprecation of technology component incentive, new NCI, deprecation of POD, PSD, and CEI incentives
 
Partner-Based Discount (Primary Focus)
The new 8% flat front-end discount replaces the previous 3% base + 4% technical capability discount structure. This discount applies to non-PPA, non-internal spend, excluding existing accounts and marketplace revenue. TD SYNNEX passes the full discount through to partners — they do not retain any portion.
Status Details:
- New Base Discount: 8% (up from 3% base; previously 3% + 4% technical competency = 7%)
- Existing Accounts (formerly "Shareshift"): Only 2% discount applies to any member account spending >$50,000/month prior to linking
- Existing Account Policy: Currently permanent (no time limit); TD SYNNEX has lobbied AWS to reduce to 1–2 years but AWS has not agreed
- First billing reflection: May bill (for April usage) will be the first to reflect the new 8% discount
- Competency benefits: Financial discount for competency is now absorbed into base rate, but competencies still provide lead generation and increased MDF access
 
Existing Accounts / Shareshift Classification
The $50,000 threshold applies at the individual member account level, not the customer or payer account as a whole. Strategies to address existing account classification were discussed.
Status Details:
- Accounts under $49,999/month at time of linking receive full channel discount
- Accounts at $50,000+ receive only 2% (existing account rate)
- Exception process available (business use case submission), but Gina noted ~80% failure rate
- Acceptable exception reasons: prior reseller went out of business, unique services the new reseller provides that the old one cannot
- Workarounds discussed:
- Modernization into new member accounts (acceptable)
- Pure lift-and-shift to new member account (frowned upon)
- Normalizing spend across member accounts before linking
- Specific Optum fintech customer noted: Has been consuming direct; team to explore business case exception with AWS to classify as base account given Optum's involvement since inception
 
DSA Migration & Timing (Moving to TD SYNNEX)
Optum is planning to move their US legal entity first, with Canada and UK as a secondary phase. The migration process and timing requirements were clarified.
Status Details:
- Process: Submit APN ID + DSA move request by region to AWS; Rupesh to provide email address
- Deadline: DSA move and CTA must be completed before end-user reporting closes (last Friday of the month — approximately March 22–23rd for April 1st effective date)
- Consent to Assign (CTA): TD SYNNEX will prepare a consolidated document with all payer IDs; Optum signs once; then a 10–15 minute call with both distributors' operations teams to transfer root access
- Important: If DSA is not moved in time, neither old nor new distributor receives the discount (street price applies)
- Canada: Rupesh remains account manager for both US and Canada regions; billing currency can be USD or CAD based on preference
- Former aggregator behavior: May attempt to retain the partner; TD SYNNEX noted competitors have dragged their feet in the past
 
New Customer Incentive (NCI)
NCI is a new program replacing POD (Partner Originated Discount), offering an additional 4% discount for 3 years on qualifying new accounts.
Status Details:
- Eligibility: Customer must have spent less than $50,000 in the prior 12 months (~$5,000/month) before linking
- Benefit: Extra 4% discount for 3 years (vs. POD's 5% for 2 years)
- Requirement: Customer must be enrolled in Billing Transfer
- Billing Transfer overview:
- Customer retains root access
- Handshake model (similar to Microsoft Azure CSP)
- Customer cannot see partner or distributor discounts
- Removes consent-to-assign requirement
- Reduces operational complexity and customer hesitation around giving up root
 
Bad Debt / Bankruptcy Protection
Optum raised concerns about customer bankruptcies and recovery options.
Status Details:
- Billing Transfer advantage: Partner can sever the handshake, stopping future liability; AWS holds remaining spend
- Infrastructure as a service is classified as a utility globally — cannot be shut off during bankruptcy protection
- TD SYNNEX process: Documents all spend, identifies legal counsel, petitions AWS for reimbursement on behalf of partners
- TD SYNNEX pays AWS every two weeks regardless of partner/customer payment status
- TD SYNNEX has successfully petitioned AWS to absorb losses in past cases
- TD SYNNEX noted as better positioned to absorb bad debt than individual partners
 
MDF (Market Development Funds)
TD SYNNEX offers an MDF match program tied to AWS MDF allocations.
Status Details:

AWS Partner Program Changes (Effective April 1st)
The meeting focused on major AWS partner program changes being rolled out, with Gina (TD SYNNEX subject matter expert) presenting a comprehensive overview. Key changes include a new base discount structure, deprecation of several legacy incentives, and new operational requirements. The changes represent a significant overhaul of how AWS incentivizes channel partners, with many updates piling up simultaneously affecting TD SYNNEX's ~4,900 global partners.
Timeline of Changes:
- January 1st: PGI started; PGD and other incentives deprecated
- February 1st: All RPPA changes went into effect
- April 1st: New base benefit, deprecation of technology component incentive, new NCI, deprecation of POD, PSD, and CEI incentives
 
Partner-Based Discount (Primary Focus)
The new 8% flat front-end discount replaces the previous 3% base + 4% technical capability discount structure. This discount applies to non-PPA, non-internal spend, excluding existing accounts and marketplace revenue. TD SYNNEX passes the full discount through to partners — they do not retain any portion.
Status Details:
- New Base Discount: 8% (up from 3% base; previously 3% + 4% technical competency = 7%)
- Existing Accounts (formerly "Shareshift"): Only 2% discount applies to any member account spending >$50,000/month prior to linking
- Existing Account Policy: Currently permanent (no time limit); TD SYNNEX has lobbied AWS to reduce to 1–2 years but AWS has not agreed
- First billing reflection: May bill (for April usage) will be the first to reflect the new 8% discount
- Competency benefits: Financial discount for competency is now absorbed into base rate, but competencies still provide lead generation and increased MDF access
 
Existing Accounts / Shareshift Classification
The $50,000 threshold applies at the individual member account level, not the customer or payer account as a whole. Strategies to address existing account classification were discussed.
Status Details:
- Accounts under $49,999/month at time of linking receive full channel discount
- Accounts at $50,000+ receive only 2% (existing account rate)
- Exception process available (business use case submission), but Gina noted ~80% failure rate
- Acceptable exception reasons: prior reseller went out of business, unique services the new reseller provides that the old one cannot
- Workarounds discussed:
- Modernization into new member accounts (acceptable)
- Pure lift-and-shift to new member account (frowned upon)
- Normalizing spend across member accounts before linking
- Specific Optum fintech customer noted: Has been consuming direct; team to explore business case exception with AWS to classify as base account given Optum's involvement since inception
 
DSA Migration & Timing (Moving to TD SYNNEX)
Optum is planning to move their US legal entity first, with Canada and UK as a secondary phase. The migration process and timing requirements were clarified.
Status Details:
- Process: Submit APN ID + DSA move request by region to AWS; Rupesh to provide email address
- Deadline: DSA move and CTA must be completed before end-user reporting closes (last Friday of the month — approximately March 22–23rd for April 1st effective date)
- Consent to Assign (CTA): TD SYNNEX will prepare a consolidated document with all payer IDs; Optum signs once; then a 10–15 minute call with both distributors' operations teams to transfer root access
- Important: If DSA is not moved in time, neither old nor new distributor receives the discount (street price applies)
- Canada: Rupesh remains account manager for both US and Canada regions; billing currency can be USD or CAD based on preference
- Former aggregator behavior: May attempt to retain the partner; TD SYNNEX noted competitors have dragged their feet in the past
 
New Customer Incentive (NCI)
NCI is a new program replacing POD (Partner Originated Discount), offering an additional 4% discount for 3 years on qualifying new accounts.
Status Details:
- Eligibility: Customer must have spent less than $50,000 in the prior 12 months (~$5,000/month) before linking
- Benefit: Extra 4% discount for 3 years (vs. POD's 5% for 2 years)
- Requirement: Customer must be enrolled in Billing Transfer
- Billing Transfer overview:
- Customer retains root access
- Handshake model (similar to Microsoft Azure CSP)
- Customer cannot see partner or distributor discounts
- Removes consent-to-assign requirement
- Reduces operational complexity and customer hesitation around giving up root
 
Bad Debt / Bankruptcy Protection
Optum raised concerns about customer bankruptcies and recovery options.
Status Details:
- Billing Transfer advantage: Partner can sever the handshake, stopping future liability; AWS holds remaining spend
- Infrastructure as a service is classified as a utility globally — cannot be shut off during bankruptcy protection
- TD SYNNEX process: Documents all spend, identifies legal counsel, petitions AWS for reimbursement on behalf of partners
- TD SYNNEX pays AWS every two weeks regardless of partner/customer payment status
- TD SYNNEX has successfully petitioned AWS to absorb losses in past cases
- TD SYNNEX noted as better positioned to absorb bad debt than individual partners

items.subject: AI is breaking per-seat pricing—now what?
items.body_text:
[George Humphrey | TSIA]
Webinar: TSIA’s practical system for profitable AI business models.
 

Connect quote-to-delivery with AI and automation. 
 View this email in your browser 

Hi Adam,

AI isn’t just adding features—it’s rewriting the economics of tech. If your plan is to “bolt on AI” and protect old pricing and delivery models, the math won’t work.
Watch the AI Economics: TSIA’s Perspective on Profitable AI Business Models webinar for an executive breakdown of what actually drives profitable AI growth—and why services are now the engine.

  

What you'll learn: 
The race to profitability. Why AI disruption is shifting where value gets captured—and why the last mile makes services unavoidable. 
How pricing is evolving. A pragmatic ladder from user-based to consumption to value/outcome pricing—and how to climb it without crushing margins. 
What “Service Portfolio 2.0” looks like. The three AI service categories—Readiness & Governance, Value Optimization, and Outcome-Oriented AI—and how they power a value engine across PS/CS/Support/MS.   

If you’re responsible for pricing, services, or growth in an AI-first world, this session will help you choose a business-model path that actually pays off.

Watch on demand 

Best,
George Humphrey
Senior Vice President of Research

            

This email was sent by: TSIA
17065 Camino San Bernardo, Suite 150 San Diego, CA, 92127, US

items.subject: ☂️ It's raining new Jira updates!
items.body_text:
[Atlassian]
Check out the newest updates to improve your Jira experience

March 2026 

TL;DR 
Agents in Jira. Seasonal releases. Connected marketing campaign playbook. Jira + Loom webinars, and more! 

What's new in Jira? 
Introducing Agents in Jira (Open Beta) 
Bring AI agents into your team's workflows. You can assign work to agents, @mention them in comments, and trigger them from workflows – so they help draft, summarize, or execute work right where it's tracked. 
 

Learn more 

New planning experiences in roadmaps 

 

Meet Jira's new seasonal release. 
We've heard your feedback for centralized Jira updates, so we've pulled everything into one place where you can see what's new and what's coming soon! Bookmark it as your go‑to hub to learn about new features and stay ahead of changes. 
See what's new 

 

Team '26 Opening Keynote guest speakers announced! 
Guess who's taking the stage in Anaheim? Hint: she's an Emmy Award‑winning journalist, author, and one of the leading voices on technology, business, and culture; and he's a leading expert on how AI is transforming the way we work and learn. Don't miss this dynamic conversation on the future of human‑AI collaboration! 
Register now 

 

Rovo Dev is now generally available in VS Code! 
The Atlassian VS Code extension already connects your code editor to Jira work items, Bitbucket pull requests, and more. Rovo Dev brings AI‑assisted development directly into the IDE (VS Code) by combining Atlassian context (Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket Cloud and Jira with Github) with code‑aware assistance. 
Get it now 

 

Ship faster with Jira + Loom 
Introducing Loom's deeper integrations with Jira: Loom's bug reporting mode now documents technical context, making it easier to capture, diagnose, and fix issues with fewer follow‑ups. Plus, AI‑suggested work item updates based on Loom meeting recordings help keep Jira boards up to date! 
Ship faster 

 

How we decide an AI feature is "good enough" to ship 
Many AI features still ship on vibes. Confluence Whiteboards PM Emily Ditchfield shares how her team reaches evidence‑based decisions using clear quality bars, an evolving eval dataset, and AI judges that scale human taste without exposing customer data. 
Read now 

 

100% off Atlassian's Teamwork Collection for Social Impact Teams 
Atlassian for Nonprofits empowers social impact teams to tackle core collaboration challenges through trusted tools, teamwork support, and peer learning. And now, Jira is available to nonprofits and social enterprises at no cost (for up to 25 users). 
Explore nonprofit 

ICYMI 
In case you missed it, here are other trending Jira stories: 

• How Schaeffler accelerates, innovates, and transforms teamwork with Atlassian. Learn more 

• Automate your workflows with Atlassian Teams in automations! Learn more 

• Turn Jira issues into code with Claude Opus 4.5 in Rovo Dev CLI. Learn more 

Upcoming events 

 

Upgrade from power users to an AI‑powered system of work 
Join us on March 31 for Teamwork in an AI era, a global digital summit, where you'll hear from Adam Grant, Atlassian product leaders, and NVIDIA's Director of Product and Engineering on how to move AI from an ad‑hoc helper into a connected system of work, while keeping teams aligned and shipping faster. 
Register now 

 

On‑demand: how our AI team ships with JPD 
Want to see how teams actually run their roadmap in Jira Product Discovery? In this on‑demand session, Atlassian's Rovo AI team walks through their live JPD setup. Learn how they use calculated fields for team‑level capacity planning, connect ideas to projects and delivery tickets so everything stays in sync, and create stakeholder‑specific views. 
Watch now 

 

Rovo Dev ships the small tasks while you tackle the cool stuff 
Join this webinar happening March 26 to see how Rovo Dev in Jira lets you spin up AI‑powered sessions from Jira work items so an AI teammate can break down the work, write and revise code with repo context, run checks, and propose ready‑to‑review PRs. 
Register now 

Visit admin hub 

See you next month! 👋 Until then... 
New to Jira? Check out the guide | Have a question? Ask the Community 
Need additional documentation? Check out Support 

Work together from anywhere with the Jira mobile app 

 
 

 

 

Read in browser • Privacy policy • Contact us • Read our blog • Join Atlassian Community 
     
Copyright 2026 Atlassian Pty Ltd. All rights reserved. 
We are located at 341 George Street, Sydney, NSW, 2000, Australia 
 

If you no longer wish to receive these emails, please update your preferences or unsubscribe here.

Hi All,

 

After some back and forths just trying to understand the options available with Beanfield, and making sure the Beanfield pricing that Matt got is competitive based on a comparative quote from Zayo, the best option is the:

 

$950 per circuit - $1900 total

  1x Dark Fiber @ 15km

  1x e-line

 

2x e-lines are more expensive. 2x Dark Fiber are $50 a month less expensive, but for diversity would require paths longer than 15km which pushes into the next range of optics and the delivery dates are much longer for DF vs. e-line. 

 

When it comes time to enter the orders I can assist with putting in these off-net services. Just let me know when we get to that point.

 

Regards,

On Mon, Mar 2, 2026 at 11:08 AM Anthony Castillo <acastillo@beanfield.com> wrote:

Salut Marc-Alexandre,  

 

Les adresses 17500 Trans Canada et 6205 Vanden Abeele sont toutes les deux des CO/POP pour nous. Les fichiers KMZ que je partage avec vous ici correspondent aux entrées des bâtiments. Notre réseau MPLS est installé directement à l'intérieur de ces bâtiments. La latence estimée est d'environ 0,4 à 0,6 ms entre les deux sites

 

Nous souhaitons profiter de cette occasion pour vous proposer une fibre noire comme alternative pour votre client. Cela lui garantirait la meilleure latence et le chemin le plus direct entre les deux sites. La fibre noire est linéaire (non protégé) et n'entre pas en contact avec notre équipement, mais la latence serait prédéterminée et ne serait pas affectée par les convergences réseau ou les coupures fibre du réseau central via MPLS.  Le client devrait simplement s'assurer d'utiliser des optiques SFP de 40 km, car la distance dépasse 10 km. La distance estimée est de 18 km entre les deux sites

 

 

Merci, 

Anthony Castillo

Ingénieur en Solutions Clients
Customer Solutions Engineer

Salut Marc-Alexandre,  

 

Les adresses 17500 Trans Canada et 6205 Vanden Abeele sont toutes les deux des CO/POP pour nous. Les fichiers KMZ que je partage avec vous ici correspondent aux entrées des bâtiments. Subject: Re: BI Report: Capture Pending Customer Approval “orders” 

 

Good Afternoon, 

 

Initial feedback is that the report seems to be pulling orders which are also below the 30 day cut-off, are we able to amend the filter to exclude those? 

JQ - Yes, I can exclude those no problem.

 

Second feedback would be that it would be preferable for this to be actioned through a Jira ticket that should automatically be assigned to the SDM queue and not via an email distro. I do a review of the ticket queue with my team every Wednesday at 9am ET so if it could be sent before 8am to align with this it would be ideal. 

JQ - I'm guessing it will be one ticket with all the data orders at once, not a ticket per order, correct?

Also, there was a mechanism that allowed me to send an email to a specific email address and a Jira ticket would be created. Is that available?

 

Since there is a number of these aged orders (~30) I'd like to know when this is going to go into effect so that I can make my team aware, and put a plan in place to address this first influx. I have already confirmed that they all have the correct permissions to retrieve and cancel orders (I do not have these permissions). 

 

Thank you,

 

Lacie Allen-Morley

Manager, Service Delivery

JP
